title: fix: deploy LTX-2 to GH200 with token auth
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: LTX-2 goes brrrrr
gist.blurb: We strapped a massive GH200 GPU to the LTX-2 video backend. Videos now generate in about 12 seconds, which is barely enough time to take a sip of coffee.
gist.summary: Migrated the LTX-2 video generation backend to a high-performance GH200 instance running a patched ComfyUI with token authentication.
gist.impact: Users will experience significantly faster video generation times, dropping to around 12-16 seconds end-to-end.
gist.visual_concept: Upgrading hardware for speed. Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot installing a massive, glowing new GPU into a retro server rack to make videos render faster.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot installing a massive, glowing lime green (#ecf874) GPU into a retro server rack. The robot's screen shows a fast-forward symbol. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
